summary: LIVE adversarial probe of the Anti-Hallucination Gateway (Ch 31) against the public preview backend with the active dolphin-mistral:free model auto-falling back to anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku. New test file backend/tests/test_gateway_live_probe.py drives gameplay through real /api/story/new + /api/story/action and inspects /api/story/session/{id}/export. Results: 2 PASSED (dead-NPC-revival, silent-injury-healing), 3 SKIPPED (destroyed-item, consumed-item, prose-state-contradiction) — the SKIPs are NOT a gateway failure but a critical integration gap: the live model never emits status='destroyed'/'consumed' in rolling_state.object_locations, so the gateway's terminal-object guard has no truth to defend. Backend log explicitly shows the hallucination re-prompt firing for the dead NPC case ('Turn validation failed (hallucination/dead NPC Garrett speaks or acts alive in the narrative) — retrying once').

critical.issue: Live model never produces rolling_state.object_locations entries with status='destroyed' or 'consumed'. Instead it (a) renames the object ('iron lantern' -> 'lantern fragments' / 'lantern powder') with location_state='dropped' and condition='broken'/'ground', and (b) silently drops consumed items from inventory_objects entirely. As a result, gateway.build_truth().terminal_objects stays EMPTY and the strip_illegal_state_changes + truth-block + prose-contradiction guards for objects NEVER FIRE against this model. The gateway only effectively defends OBJECTS when the model itself sets status=destroyed/consumed (or when seeded that way in tests). Repro: explore script /tmp/explore_terminal.py — after 3 unambiguous destroy/consume actions, rolling_state.object_locations was null and inventory_objects showed only fragments/dust at location_state=dropped, condition=broken.

minor.issue: Under load the preview returned a transient HTTP 502 'The preview environment is not responding'. Health recovered automatically. Not a gateway bug; flag only if rate of 502 grows.

action_items: [CRITICAL] Make terminal-object detection independent of the LLM's `status` field. Two complementary fixes: (1) In server.py state-merge, when inventory_objects[].location_state in {destroyed,consumed} OR condition in {broken,shattered,ash,charred,melted,ground,crumbled,empty,eaten,drained} AND prior_rolling carried the parent object, synthesise an object_locations row with status='destroyed' (or 'consumed' for ingestibles). (2) Mirror gateway.update_death_registry — add a gateway.update_destruction_registry that scans prose+player_action for unambiguous destruction/consumption verbs against KNOWN inventory names and writes status=destroyed/consumed into merged_rolling.object_locations before the next turn. Both can coexist.

[HIGH] Strengthen the system prompt / contract: when an item is irreversibly destroyed or consumed in-scene, the model MUST emit an object_locations row with status='destroyed' or 'consumed' (not just rename it or remove it from inventory). Add an example in the prose-to-state schema description.

[MEDIUM] Add a state-guard alias: treat inventory_objects[*].condition in {broken,shattered,ash,ruined} as terminal when prior turns establish the same parent identity, so the engine doesn't allow the model to later restore the item by renaming it back.

[LOW] Document in PRD.md that the LIVE pass-rate of the object-terminal guard depends on the model emitting the trigger format, and that injuries/deceased guards work end-to-end with the current model (verified).
critical_code_review_comments: gateway.py TERMINAL_OBJECT_STATUSES={'destroyed','consumed'} is correct in spirit but the live model never reaches this status without server-side normalisation. The unit + e2e tests pass because they hand-feed status='destroyed'; the real model takes a different path that the gateway is blind to.

Death registry (update_death_registry) works against the live model — verified by backend log line 'Turn validation failed (hallucination/dead NPC Garrett speaks or acts alive in the narrative) — retrying once'. Confirms the prose-keyword path is robust.
Silent injury healing guard works end-to-end — verified by test_silent_injury_healing_blocked passing without the model trying to silently heal a severe injury under a non-recovery action.
The hallucination re-prompt is observably wired in correctly (retry triggered for dead NPC speaking).

success_rate.backend: 2/5 live probes PASSED, 3/5 SKIPPED on missing trigger condition. 0 FAILED. Deterministic suite (test_anti_hallucination_gateway.py + test_gateway_e2e.py) remains green.

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral probe-<uuid> sessions created and DELETEd in each test's teardown. No persistent seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 3 SKIPPED probes (destroyed item, consumed item, prose-vs-state on objects) are blocked at the trigger step, not at the gateway step: the live model under the current prompt does not emit status='destroyed'/'consumed' in rolling_state.object_locations. /tmp/explore_terminal.py is a 3-turn reproduction that prints exactly what object_locations / inventory_objects / ledger / debug fields the model produces after smash+stomp+eat actions; rerun it to confirm before/after any fix. The dead-NPC and silent-injury probes are stable PASSes against the real model; treat them as the working baseline. Each live turn costs 20-45s (rate-limited fallback to claude-3-5-haiku); allocate ~6-9 min for the full probe file.
rca_of_issue: Reproduction: POST /api/story/new -> POST /api/story/action with 'smash the iron lantern until it shatters' (x2) -> GET /api/story/session/{id}/export. Observed: rolling_state.object_locations is null, inventory_objects shows {object:'lantern fragments', condition:'broken', location_state:'dropped'}. Expected (for gateway to engage): a row in object_locations with status='destroyed' and object identity matching 'iron lantern'. Root cause: the model's structured-state output for irreversible destruction takes a rename+condition path that the gateway is not configured to recognise. Mitigation (server.py): in the state-merge / state_guard step, detect destruction-shaped renames (parent name -> '<name> fragments|shards|ash|powder|husk' OR condition in {broken,shattered,ash,charred,burnt,melted}) and synthesise an object_locations row with status='destroyed' under the ORIGINAL identity, then let the gateway take over for subsequent turns. Symmetric handling for consumption (inventory_objects entry simply disappearing) should be done by scanning the player action + narrative for consume verbs (eat/drink/swallow/finish) against the previously-known inventory and writing status='consumed' under the original identity.
